body.title: Fix test_stratified_split_approximately_twenty_percent — fragile row-count assumption
body.result: Fixed: changed absolute holdout count assertion to ratio check (10%–30% tolerance). 3/3 tests pass. Commit 5467f23b.
body.intention: I intend that the holdout test validates proportional split logic without depending on exact DB row counts.
body.description:
test_stratified_split_approximately_twenty_percent asserts holdout <= 3 but actual data has more than 10 rows per type, causing the test to get 6 holdout rows and fail.

The test was written assuming exactly 10 rows per type in the competition.memories DB. Actual data has grown beyond that. Fix: either mock the data layer to return exactly N rows, or change the assertion to check a ratio (holdout / total) rather than an absolute count.

**Affected files:** tests/competition/test_carve_holdout.py
**Design rules:** no mocks on DB — use a fixture with controlled input, or assert ratio
**Scope boundary:** IN — fix the single failing assertion. OUT — changing the carve logic itself.
**Completion criteria:** python -m pytest tests/competition/test_carve_holdout.py passes on CI regardless of actual DB row count.
